name: Benjamin Legal Services
description_short: Chicago-based law firm specializing in bankruptcy, foreclosure defense, and tax resolution with 35+ years of attorney experience.
description_long:
Benjamin Legal Services, PLC was established in 1989 and is based in Chicago's West Loop neighborhood at 1016 W. Jackson Blvd. The firm is led by J. Kevin Benjamin, Esq., an attorney with over 35 years of experience in financial law. The firm has spent three decades assisting both individuals and businesses navigate complex financial challenges, with a focus on helping clients achieve financial stability and fresh starts.

The firm offers a comprehensive range of services across three primary practice areas: bankruptcy law (Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings), foreclosure solutions and defense, and tax resolution services involving IRS negotiations. Beyond core bankruptcy and foreclosure work, Benjamin Legal Services also handles student loan debt management, loan workouts for borrowers in default, and mortgage restructuring to help clients renegotiate terms and make payments more manageable. All initial consultations are offered free with no obligation, and the firm provides flexible payment plans to make legal services accessible.

What distinguishes Benjamin Legal Services is the depth of experience—35+ years from the principal attorney—and the firm's focus on personalized, affordable solutions. They emphasize accessibility through multiple communication channels (phone, video conference, and in-person meetings), convenient West Loop location near public transit, and explicit affordable payment plan options. The firm positions itself as a full-service financial law provider rather than a single-issue practice, addressing bankruptcy alongside foreclosure defense and tax issues.

Honestly assessed, Benjamin Legal Services appears to be a legitimate, established law firm with transparent credentials and clear service offerings. The website provides specific contact information, business hours, and physical location. However, potential clients should note that legal outcomes in bankruptcy and foreclosure cases depend heavily on individual circumstances, and the firm's marketing emphasizes their experience and affordability but provides limited specific case results or client testimonials (though reviews section exists). As with any legal services, clients should verify licensing and conduct independent research before engagement.
pros: Principal attorney has 35+ years of documented experience in bankruptcy, foreclosure, and tax law
Free no-obligation initial consultation with no cost to explore options
Offers affordable payment plans to make legal services accessible to those in financial distress
Serves both individuals and businesses, providing comprehensive financial law solutions beyond just bankruptcy
Multiple contact methods available: phone, video conference, and in-person meetings at West Loop office
Convenient location near public transit (UIC-Halstead and Racine Metro Stations) and major Chicago neighborhoods
Established firm since 1989 with three decades of client service history
cons: Website displays limited specific client testimonials or case results to verify outcomes
Only operates Monday-Friday 9am-6pm with no weekend hours, limiting accessibility for working clients
Primary practice focus on bankruptcy and foreclosure may mean less specialization in some services (e.g., student loan law) compared to dedicated firms
No information provided about attorney licensing verification, bar standing, or disciplinary history on the website itself
Marketing language emphasizes 'affordable' plans but provides no specific fee schedules, hourly rates, or cost ranges for different services
best_for: Chicago-area individuals or small business owners facing bankruptcy and needing experienced local legal representation
Homeowners in foreclosure seeking experienced defense counsel in Illinois
People with IRS tax issues and back taxes who need resolution strategies combined with bankruptcy planning
diagnosis: Benjamin Legal Services is best for Chicago-area residents or business owners facing bankruptcy, foreclosure, or serious tax issues who want experienced local counsel (35+ years) and need affordable payment options. The main caveat is that outcomes in bankruptcy and foreclosure depend heavily on individual circumstances and state law, and the website lacks specific case results, client testimonials, or fee transparency to fully evaluate value before initial consultation.
services: Tax resolution and IRS negotiation services
Student loan debt management and restructuring advice
Loan workouts for borrowers in default on mortgages
Mortgage restructuring and renegotiation with lenders
Payment plan financing for legal fees
